Some even have the nerve to defy the will of Tunare and kill bears in Surefall Glade! Tunare is the Mother of All. It was she who cultivated the seed of humanity in the ancient soil of Norrath.

speaker: Bren Treeclimber
dialog: Hello, Heh? Oh. Poachers are constantly after our bears for their valuable skins. I wish those darn [Millers] would move on. Then, maybe, our bears would be left alone. I have seen far too many of my friends die. I will kill anyone I see harming my bears. The Millers?! Those butchers! We have caught that Baobob and his sister trying to poach more than once. And to think we took them in and gave them shelter when they were in need. All the while, they were just trying to get to our bears. Mammoth is the oldest bear in these caves. Many poachers have tried to part him from his hide. Many poachers have met their fates at the end of my blade, too. Talym Shoontar is a wanted man. He is a very infamous poacher. Hager Sureshot has placed a bounty upon his head. Hey there, big bear! Good bear! Keep clear of those mean ol' poachers.

speaker: Joffrey Clay
dialog: Greetings, friend. My name is Joffrey. I'm trying to learn to play the [flute]. I'm also working on a new song I learned while I was visiting [Kelethin]. I just got back from a trip to Faydwer. I was over there studying the lore of Tunare, in the beautiful elven city of Kelethin. I highly recommend visiting there as soon as you get a chance. You like my flute? Jusean made it for me. His father was a master instrument craftsman, and Jusean learned his technique very well. He should be around here somewhere. He's kinda lazy, so I imagine he wouldn't stray too far. Belious, I'm working on this new tune I learned on my last trip to Kelethin. It's about Tunare. If you have a free ear, would you care to take a listen? Ahem. Tunare and the Pact, sung by Joffrey Clay. La da di, la di da.. la da di. la di da..

speaker: Moran Smith
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! Greetings!! Moran here. I just love this city. I traveled from Freeport to Qeynos to stay far from the [Freeport Militia]. The Freeport Militia are nothing more than thugs. I have seen them lay waste to more than a dozen adventurers at once. For no reason other than to flex their muscles! Commander Kane Bayle is the commander of all the Qeynos Guard. He is second only to his brother, Antonius Bayle. His post is in the guard house at the city gates. Mind you, do not bother him, he has a bit of a temper. The Circle of the Unseen Hand? I have heard nothing of them. Are they some sort of performing magic troupe? I am sorry. I have no extra currency to donate.

speaker: Tralyn Marsinger
dialog: Hail, The League of Antonican Bards has a courier system made up of travelers, adventurers and [agents]. We pay good gold to anyone who will take messages from bards such as myself to one of our more distant offices. Are you [interested]? Sivina Lutewhisper, Marton Stringsinger, Ticar Lorestring and Mistrana Two-Notes all report to me. Marton Stringsinger lives with the barbarians in the city of Halas. Sivina Lutewhisper lives with the rangers in Surefall Glade. Ticar Lorestring sings for the people of the city of Erudin. Mistrana Two-Notes is currently visiting with the McMannus tribe in the Karanas. Postmistress Felisity Stabright lives in the city of Freeport. Postmistress Lislia Goldtune lives in the fortress of High Keep. Incoming mail - very good! Please take this gold for your troubles.

speaker: Vin Moltor
dialog: Heh?? .. BURRP!.. Leave me be will ya. Paw ya say? Yeah.. Yeah .. I got this ratty old thing still. You want it? Its yours for a Bottle of Kalish and hm.. .. How bad you want it? Hm.. Two.. No no.. Three Gold.. Er.. NO! Wait. Pie.. Yes! Rat Ear Pie is what I want.. Three Bottles of Kalish and a Rat Eat Pie. If you want the Paw that is my price. Rings? I don't know anything about no rings... My [father] prob'ly sold them or lost them. They could be anywhere. My father was a drunk and a loser just like me. He traveled all of Antonica, though. He even went to Odus once or twice, I think. He died a long time ago. Ah. Come to ol'Vin my dear sweet Kalish.. Mmmm.. And Rat Ear Pie.. I am going to eat and drink like a KING! Huh? Oh.. The Paw.. Here you go.. Hope it brings you more luck then it did me..

speaker: Talym Shoontar
dialog: How are you, friend? It is a good day to be alive in Antonica! If you should happen to visit Surefall Glade, say hello to Hager Sureshot for me. Hager Sureshot is that know-nothing ranger in charge of the Protectors of the Pine. He thinks he can protect all of Surefall Glade. He is wrong. Excuse me! I am a HUNTER. A hunter of profit, not glory. Have you come to [collect the bounty] on my head? For your sake, the answer had better be [no]. Then do your best, whelp! I need the practice. I hope for your sake that my gypsy friends are not nearby. Too bad. I heard there is a new market for skins of your kind. Surefall Glade is in the Jaggedpine Forest. Home to the druids and rangers. Home to some of the finest bearskins ever to walk the earth. I can hear the coins clinking already.

speaker: Vesteri Nomanoi
dialog: Hail, I need you to take this claim check to Qeynos for me. Nesiff Talaherd is a woodcarver who owns a shop in South Qeynos. I am having him carve a small statue of Tunare for Te'Anara. With my training schedule, I can not make the journey to Qeynos. Please give this claim check to Nesiff and bring the statue to me. It is a surprise, so please do not say anything to Te'Anara about it. We, the Jaggedpine Treefolk, appreciate the help you've given us in the past. But, we must trust you more before allowing you to handle such important matters. You are an enemy of the Jaggedpine Treefolk, this forest, and the residents of it. Begone, before I am forced to take drastic measures! Oh thank you so much! Here. Take this reward for your time. I will also tell Te'Anara of you.

speaker: Vicus Nonad
dialog: coughs and wheezes. Greetings, Oh, <cough> I am sorry, but it seems I have fallen a bit ill. I was caught out in the rain the other day and my chills have gotten the best of me. <cough> If only someone would [help] me with today's [collections].. <cough> Oh thank <cough> you so <cough> <cough> much <cough>.. Here is the official collection box. Please collect from each merchant on the <cough> [list]. Then bring me back the combined total of all your collections. Oh. <cough> I am sorry.. I forgot to give it to you. Here you go. Be sure to give that back when your job is finished. <cough> <cough> Great! Thank you so much. Here is a small gratuity for a job well done. Thank you again. <cough> Antonius Bayle and the People of Qeynos appreciate all yo have done.

speaker: Jracol Brestiage
dialog: Look what I found! I hear there's a good market for these back in Highpass.. lucky me! You'd better run along, if ya know what's best for ya! Carson hates you, and I hate you... I'd kill you where you stand, but I just polished my dagger last night. Yes, it is a lovely night for a walk, especially with a good friend. Tell me, are you a [friend]? Carson hates you, and I hate you... I'd kill you where you stand, but I just polished my dagger last night. My memory is a bit fuzzy. If you are a friend, prove it to me. Carson hates you, and I hate you... I'd kill you where you stand, but I just polished my dagger last night. 'Ah, good, it seems we have much in common after all. Take this back to the Circle, before the city guards come nosing around over here.

speaker: Bait Masterson
dialog: You know.. I took up fishing because it was a nice, quiet activity. Most of all, I took it up to avoid conversing with strangers. Get the picture?!! Prexus!!? Aye!! I once followed the ways of the Ocean Lord. I remember those days. So clear and peaceful were they. I shall make a deal with you, my friend. I shall give up fishing if you give me your guild tunic. With such a tunic I shall once again be compelled to follow the peaceful ways of the Ocean Lord. If I were to donate, it would not be to a temple which follows the Rainkeeper. There is no greater deity than Prexus, the Oceanlord. Nice material!! I feel the ways of Prexus enlightening my soul. Unngh!! Enough of this fishing. Here take my broken fishing pole and toss it to the sea. All hail Prexus!!

speaker: Merdan Fleetfoot
dialog: Yes Niera! Oh. Erm. Hi. I mean, uh. How are you? blushes. 'She is, uh.. I don't know. I haven't been able to speak with her. She has many suitors, and I'm just another poor ranger. I spend my days here pretending to fish, to carve bows, but really I'm just waiting to catch a glimpse of her. I thought about adventuring into the world to find some kind of token to give her, to show her how I feel, but I can't tear myself away from here.' blinks and looks at the flower. 'This is perfect. Thank you! I'm going to go over to her now. Erm, well, maybe I'll wait a week to let the uh.. beauty of the flower.. uhm.. blossom. Yes, that sounds good. Here, you take this painting. I don't need it now. I made it myself, she wouldn't like it anyways.'

speaker: Nesiff Tallaherd
dialog: Greetings, Guard Weleth is like a son to me. He is responsible for purchasing supplies for the Qeynos Guard. He buys arrows from me. He is usually stationed near the North Gate. Tax time again already?? It seems like I just paid yesterday.. High taxes and low sales mean I won't be able to stay in business much longer. Good thing ol' Weleth is taking care of me. Here are my taxes. Oh dear. I hope my mistake will not bring too much grief down on poor Weleth. He was told not to order arrows from me after the last time I fouled up their shipment. But Weleth knew I needed the account to stay in business. Could you please rush this new invoice to him right away? Thank you. Oh. Vesteri sent you? Here you go. I hope Te'Anara likes it.

speaker: Sneed Galliway
dialog: Hello, Shhh.. Keep it down. It seems I have a little rodent problem in my store. A mangy rat keeps digging into my crates of rations. He looks as if he is diseased. If you help me by killing this pest, I will make it worth your while. Bring me his head as proof it is done. Ronn? I saw him a few days ago. He was with someone dressed in black I didn't recognize .. I think I saw them later that day, swimming in the north pond. They really should stay away from the aqueducts. They can be quite dangerous. Full of rats, ya know. Hey, HA! Good work! I knew you were the person for the job. A regular ratslayer you are. Here is your reward as promised. be sure you keep this between us.

speaker: Barn Bloodstone
dialog: You seem a little out of place here. You should go back to the nice little village you came from. This place will eat you alive. The [Golden Rooster] is no place for you. Word from the grapevine says to use you as a pincushion. Just consider yourself lucky all I am doing is not speaking to you. Get lost!! Blackburrow Stout is the finest drink available. It is the nectar of the gods. Well.. nectar of the gnolls at least. I can't wait until they hold another drink special in the Rooster. When I hear that. I am there! The Golden Rooster only happens to be the finest gambling house in Highpass. It is also the only place in these parts that serves [Blackburrow Stout].

speaker: an investigator
dialog: coughs and struggles to move. 'There were... too many... too strong.' gasps clutching a wound and says, 'Their magic was too powerful. Necromancers. Undead. Cursed knights of an unholy god. They left when they thought I was dead. But even now their curses sap the last of my life. I made it as far as I could. If Helminth lives, tell him [I am dead]. Please complete the mission. Bring this Azibelle to justice. Avenge me.' Vegalys sent you. I'm done for. Guard Helminth found a false wall. We stepped through it. We were ambushed. I didn't see where Helminth went. He disappeared. I knew we were overwhelmed. I ran and feigned death but I knew [it was too late].

speaker: Raz The Rat Misk
dialog: Spare 3 gold so I can get a decent meal for once, master? Beat it. You ain't part of the Circle. I found this pouch and do not know whose it is. I would be more than willing to part with it for 5 gold, since the owner would probably reward you more. Beat it. You ain't part of the Circle. Very good, master! Best of luck in locating the proper owner. Maybe try giving it to ol' Crow in North Qeynos. Everyone knows if you need to fence.. er.. find something in Qeynos, Crow is your man. He always seems to know who's missing what. I am off to get some food. Thank you, kind master. I don't wish to trouble you further but I do have another [favor] to ask..

speaker: Aenia Ghenson
dialog: Why, hello there! I'm Aenia, daughter of Dranom. It's such a bore just sitting around here all day and night. My father is soooooo overprotective! He never lets me go out and have any fun at all. And he's so mean to my poor [boyfriend]! My boyfriend's name is Behroe Dlexon. He is so dreamy.. <sigh>.. But, I haven't been able to see him lately. He works nights down at the docks, and with my father here all day, we just can't seem to get together. Oh, how I wish I could speak with him.. Heh.. With all you've done, I'm surprised you're still alive. Oh.. My Behroe is so sweet. Oh, how I long to see him. Please, oh please, kind

speaker: Talym Shoontar
dialog: How are you, friend? It is a good day to be alive in Antonica! If you should happen to visit Surefall Glade, say hello to Hager Sureshot for me. Hager Sureshot is that know-nothing ranger in charge of the Protectors of the Pine. He thinks he can protect all of Surefall Glade. He is wrong. Excuse me! I am a HUNTER. A hunter of profit, not glory. Have you come to [collect the bounty] on my head? For your sake, the answer had better be [no]. Then do your best, whelp! I need the practice. I hope for your sake that my gypsy friends are not nearby. Too bad. I heard there is a new market for skins of your kind.

speaker: Axe Broadsmith
dialog: Greetings. The fishing is sparse here. You would fare better at [Lake Rathe]. Beyond the Plains of Karana can be found a pass to Lake Rathe. I have heard stories of a giant water beast and underwater cities. I wish I knew how to swim. So you are the new warrior. Let us test your skill. Across the pond is a skeleton. See him? Engage him in combat. He awaits. Return his skull to me and I shall call you a Steel Warrior. Be quick. He will dissipate soon. So you have returned. Victory is yours, young Steel Warrior. Take this letter of recommendation to Brin Stolunger at the arena in Qeynos. You have passed.

speaker: Solani Dayadil
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! If you are a fellow merchant. I must warn you of the inevitable tax increase. Antonius Bayle is soon to make a [deal] with the people of Surefall Glade. Bayle is soon to sign a law which will raise the taxes on all bear hides. This is to keep the purchase of the hides to a minimum. All this to keep the treehuggers happy!! Did you not hear about the [deal] with Surefall Glade?! I will have little money to EAT, let alone donate to another person's cause!

speaker: Teydar
dialog: Hello The object you desire is very difficult to fashion. Nearly impossible, really. All I can offer to do is try. The real difficulty is the contents that give the sheath its power. They have to be of an almost equal evil. I think some kind of decrepit hide and a drake spine, along with a bar of enchanted platinum, should prove enough to make this item. I did it! The pieces you got me were perfect! It took a huge effort on my part to craft this, of course. It saddens me that I'm unable to keep one of my best works, but a deal is a deal. There is honor among shadowknights.

speaker: Flynn Merrington
dialog: Oh look.. another gnoll pup. This bar is too rough for the likes of you. Why don't you try Fish's. We don't need any more regulars here, chump! Ha! Yeah, I took their tax money. What are you going to do about it, little gnoll pup? Tell you want.. I will cut you a deal. You tell me you are a [gnoll loving weakling who isn't fit to comb my feet] and I will give you that merchant's tax money. BWAH! HA HA HA! Did you all hear that?! Well, now... That's cutting out the middle man. Thanks, chum! Now, beat it! HA! You Erudites never could handle your liquor. Go feed the fish.

speaker: Lars McMannus
dialog: Hello. The name's Lars McMannus of Clan McMannus. Halas is the mighty city of the barbarians of the North. It lies among the Everfrost Peaks. Frostbite is our trusty wolf. We found him as a pup. He is my brother and the oldest of my siblings. She is my sister. He is my father. We hail from Halas. She is my sister and the youngest of the clan. He is my brother and a fine bowyer and net maker. He is my brother. The Wolves of the North are the barbarian warriors of Halas. All of us in Clan McMannus are members. Mess with us and you mess with the Wolves of the North.

speaker: Wyle Bimlin
dialog: picks an item up from the ground and says, 'Hey! Look what I found! Another piece of rubbish for my trailer... Must be my lucky day. Greetings, fine friend! I bet you are in need of some fine tonics. Feeling a little low? Take a drink of my fine Zap Doodle Tonic. It will give you the energy of five hill giants! You are in luck, my friend! I have it on sale today, and today only. Matter of fact, the sale ends in five minutes or five footsteps, whichever comes first. So don't be down... get an upside-down frown with my Zap Doodle Tonic! All sales are final.

speaker: Earron Huntlan
dialog: Greetings, thirsty adventurer! Please help yourself to the free salty snacks. We are here to serve you at the Lion's Main Tavern. Allow yourself to indulge from our wide selection oof fine spirits. Kwint? Gave you the line about being my brother, huh? That boy sends me more customers, I tells ya! I give him one free drink for every person he gets to come in. Hey, could you do me a favor and take this voucher to him when you are done drinking? Thanks. What the..? This voucher was for Kwint, not you! I will find someone more trustworthy to take it to him.

speaker: Zaen Kalystir
dialog: Welcome to the Shrine of the Diseased! You will be repaid for your service when your soul crosses the void into Bertoxxulous' waiting arms. Are you available to [do His bidding], or are you [too busy]? That is good. We need you to go and speak with [Commander Kane Bayle] of the Qeynos Militia. You will find him at the gatehouse. Tell him that the disease is spreading. Now go. Commander Kane Bayle is a new member of the Shrine of Bertoxxulous. He will assist us in turning the city of Qeynos from thunder to plague. Then be off and spread the disease.

speaker: Hurrieta Ironforge
dialog: What are you doing back here?! I cannot stand it when the [children] allow their friends to run rampant on our estate. Please leave! My dear sweet children are working in the storefronts. Well, all of them except for [Tayla]. Tayla! She is no Ironforge. Waleana is a stain on Brohan's life. A horrid elf. She is gone now and Brohan has moved onto better things and more sanity. Oh! You are here to see Brohan. My husband is upstairs. Feel free to pay him a visit, considering you have already set foot on our estate. My roses are growing quite well.

speaker: Tubal Weaver
dialog: Hey! Someone dropped a perfectly good thing-a-ma-jig! Oh well, finders keepers! Hail, Whew. I am parched. I will be back in a few minutes. Hey sweetheart, I'll have my usual. Could you bring it upstairs for me? Hey, hey, hey! Tubal Weaver open for business! Is he wise to us? Well, we won't have to worry about him much longer. I have some friends coming into town who owe me the kind of debt you can't repay with gold. Don't you worry, sugar, his days are numbered. You better get back before he suspects something. We'll be together soon enough.

speaker: Guard Leopold
dialog: Hail, Why, yes! I would love to donate to the Temple of Thunder. My father was a brave and noble member of that order. Here you are. You should ask Guard Cheslin also. His father and mine are both members of Thunder. Now, move along! Blast those dogs! The Sabertooths is what they call themselves around here. They have a nest, or whatever you call it, up there east of Surefall Glade. We can always use help here, keeping those dirty gnolls away from our fine city. Talk to Captain Tillin in South Qeynos and tell him you want to join the fight.

speaker: Karn Tassen
dialog: Ooh yeah! So would I, honey. Hello Commander Kane Bayle is the commander of all the Qeynos Guard. He is second only to his brother, Antonius Bayle. His post is in the guard house at the city gates. Mind you, do not bother him, he has a bit of a temper. The Circle of the Unseen Hand? I have heard nothing of them. Are they some sort of performing magic troupe? Hey, thanks kid! You are one top notch baker! Here's some coin for your trouble. Ah finally, fresh bread! All right let me get this on the shelves right away. Here is your payment.

speaker: Yakem Oreslinger
dialog: Greetings friend I need you to take this crate and fill it with either Small Bracks of Unrefined Ore or Large Bricks of Unrefined Ore then return it to me so that I may refine it and sell it. The unrefined ore that I require is often mined by the goblins in the ruins of Permafrost Keep. Thank you! I will prepare the ore for sale and be open for business again momentarily. Here is your payment for your services. Thank you! I will prepare the ore for sale and be open for business again momentarily. Here is your payment for your services.

speaker: Sabnie Blagard
dialog: Greetings. thirsty traveler! I recommend trying Crow's special brew. It's the brew that put Qeynos on the map! Did someone order an ale up here? Tubal doesn't suspect a thing.. the poor sap. Um, sure. Be right.. um.. up. Yes. Fine. That guy just gives me the creeps is all. No. No. He is clueless. I've missed you so. Please be careful. I don't know what I would do if anything happened to you. No! um.. no. That's ok. He's just a little weird, that's all. Nothing to worry about. Hee hee hee! Oh.. how sad! HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE!

speaker: Nindle Hogster
dialog: Hello! You should take up fishing. It really relieves the stress that accompanies heavy adventuring. First someone asks me to donate to Prexus and now this. Don't you people have better things to do than beg from us hard working citizens? Prexus!? I should have figured you for one of those Prexus fanatics. Why don't you go take a dip in the harbor?! I hear the sharks are hungry. You should take a trip to the aqueducts. You might find a fellow Prexus praiser named Bait Masterson. He loves to sneak down there at night.

speaker: Kazlo Naedra
dialog: Hail, These blank sheets seem harder to find every month. All the local merchants are out of them. Hopefully, one of the merchants out in Surefall will have some. Yes, we're almost out of [blank scroll sheets]. Gahlith will be very upset, unless I can replenish our inventory soon. The Order of Three has been monitoring your recent activities, and we are appalled by you and your actions! Now, begone! Good work, Hey guys, you got any blank scroll sheets for sale? Or know where I could get some around here?

speaker: Sivina Lutewhisper
dialog: The League of Antonican Bards has a courier system made up of travelers and adventurers. We pay good gold to anyone who will take messages from bards such as myself to one of our more central offices. Are you [interested]? I have messages that need to go to - well, right now I have one that needs to go to Qeynos. Will you [deliver] mail to [Qeynos] for me? Take this letter to Tralyn Marsinger in Qeynos. You can find him at the bard guild hall. I am sure he will compensate you for your troubles.

speaker: Rodgar the Tall
dialog: Greetings, What is all this talk of Treant Wood? I can get you some. In about two months, I shall have a new stock. Perhaps I may have some to trade... if you do me a [favor]... I will be glad to part with some Treant Wood for an Odus Pearl from the waters surrounding Erudin. On second thought... make that four Odus Pearls. Donate!? Ha! With the taxes imposed by Antonius Bayle, I have no coins to donate to ANY guild! Wonderful!! I am sure that was a lot safer than battling a treant.

speaker: Marlin Bizmite
dialog: Greetings, fellow fisherman. You must be a fisherman. You have the stench of one. If you are not, might I suggest this new pastime I have heard of? A bath!! Oh no!! Not another Prexus freak!! Listen up aqua man, if you think a man named Marlin has any chance of giving up fishing, you have got another thing coming. Go night diving in the north pond, you might find some bait for your Prexus blessing. Please! Can't you pester the Ironforges? They are the richest family in Qeynos.

speaker: LuSun
dialog: Ah.. Greetings.. I am Lu'Sun, Guild Master of the Silent Fist Clan. Here, our goal is to train our body, mind, and soul to the peak of perfection. Tomer? I think Seta needs someone to go find him.. Go ask her. Greetings, and welcome to the Silent Fist Clan. Don our guild tunic, purge your mind and soul, and begin your lifelong devotion to the Clan. Brother Esrinap will introduce you to our lifestyle and begin your training. Be sound, allow your spirit to open up and guide you.

speaker: Tol Nicelot
dialog: I have no time to waste with strangers! Yes, I knew Moodoro Finharn. He used to be a good friend until he started visiting Qeynos and indulging in human vices. He was executed for crimes against the state. Not even his sister Nolusia could get the charge dropped. There have been some who say he escaped execution and lives in Qeynos. That is insane babbling. Nolusia is a member of the Craftkeepers. She lives as a guildmaster in Erudin Palace.

speaker: Sragg Bloodheart
dialog: I will hear no more excuses, Tovax! Enjoy eternal pain and suffering! Ah, another would-be soldier for the army of the Plaguebringer. Unmuz has just lost a few of his men, he'll have plenty of work for you. I'm sure. Begone! What have you brought me, worm? Hmm... This is disappointing. That Tovax has failed us for the last time. Your loyalty to Bertoxxulous and the Bloodsabers has been noted. Now, if you will excuse me a moment...

speaker: Klieb Torne
dialog: Can you believe that? That guy actually thinks he is going to find the [Paw of Opolla!]! Bwah ha ha ha! Hey, Commander Kane Bayle is the commander of all the Qeynos Guard. He is second only to his brother, Antonius Bayle. His post is in the guard house at the city gates. Mind you, do not bother him, he has a bit of a temper. Heh? Why don't you mind your own business bub?! Buzz off! Shut your hole, Micc, you dirty son of a kobold!

speaker: Gnasher Furgutt
dialog: Who are you? Did McNeal send you? If not, you would do yourself good to leave Gnasher alone. I have friends in high places. I don't believe you. Now, leave! I know of you! You leave before Gnasher calls gnoll watchers. You are no friend of gnolls or Gnasher's human friends... Wrong answer! Ah. Good for you! Here you are. Take this to McNeal, but next time there will be no stout if there are no weapons.

speaker: Dranom Ghenson
dialog: Hello there, Aenia is my beautiful daughter. Many so-called men have tried to win her hand in marriage, but none of them have been able to live up to my standards. My daughter shall have a nobleman or a wealthy merchant... certainly not some lowly bard, like that Behroe she's been seeing. If I ever catch him, or one of his cronies, around my daughter again, I'll bash their good-for-nothing heads in.

speaker: Mespha Tevalian
dialog: Greetings.. I am Mespha Tevalian, Master Enchantress of the Hall of Sorcery and the Order of Three. Here, we all study the three circles of magic, and lend our services to citizens of Qeynos. Rumors of an underground group calling themselves the Bloodsabers have recently been spreading throughout the city. It is our duty to help local guards protect Qeynos' citizens from this new evil. Greetings

speaker: Meera Lylon
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! Hello. I am afraid I cannot tell you much about my merchandise. I was hired by Ton to look after the shop at night. I can tell you that you would find a better deal during the daytime hours. Ton instructs me to charge more for nighttime service.

speaker: Largon Welsh
dialog: Grab a pole and start fishing. You never know what you will catch. Rohan is offering a reward for some fish. Dead or alive! Donate!? I think not. Go ask [Bait Masterson]. He has made a bundle from selling his daily catch. Masterson? Lately, I hear he has been spending a lot of time fishing in the catacombs underneath Qeynos. Must be at night when his wife is asleep. Ha!

speaker: Iala Lenard
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! Welcome to my humble establishment! I specialize in cloth, but lately I have begun to dabble in silk, the finest material I have ever used. Hold on! Once I hear the word 'donate,' that is where the conversation ends.

speaker: Faldor Hendrys
dialog: What do you want?! Can't you see I am busy drinking here? You don't even look like you belong here. You can have my seat when I am good and ready to leave, which won't be for a while yet. If I were you, I would get lost before I got myself hurt. Listen, buddy! I do NOT know what you are babbling about! If you know what's good for you, then you'd best leave.

speaker: Daenor Casthopur
dialog: Ah, hello there, Neclo Rheslar is an old friend of mine. Right now, he's out working in the Qeynos Hills. In fact, if you have some free time, you should go out and meet him. Here, if you go, take this note to him for me. Thanks, friend. The Order of Three has been monitoring your recent activities, and we are appalled by you and your actions! Now, begone!

speaker: Leanon Ruksey
dialog: Ah, good day, friend. My name's Leanon Ruksey, loyal member of the League of Antonican Bards. Qeynos Chapter, of course. It's part of my job to keep up with the goings-on of the city, who's coming and going and all that. So we usually keep a man here and one at the docks. Anyway, nice chatting with you. Be careful out there! Ah, hello there,

speaker: Gahna Salbeen
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! Greetings. This is a grand city. I plan to make much profit here. Qeynos is the place where I will make a fortune. In order to do that, I must hoard all my coins and not donate to ancient religions.

speaker: Svena Ironforge
dialog: Welcome to Ironforge Jewelers. We are the finest jewelers this side of the Serpent's Spine. Tayla is our poor, dear, sweet half-sister. She has run away to live with those closer to [her kind]. Her kind is elven. Tayla is a halfbreed. She could no longer take the torment by the human populace of Qeynos. Poor girl. I feel for her.

speaker: Reania Jukle
dialog: Greetings, Ink of the Dark, you say? That isn't an everyday item, you know. In fact, I can't remember the last time someone requested it. I have given up keeping any here with me. You are going to need to find your own supply now. Sorry. The ink is the blood of a dark scribe. Tempt him and give him this vial. He should cooperate.

speaker: Voleen Tassen
dialog: Oh, it's so nice to see you again Oh dear. We had forgotten about those pesky taxes. Here you go, Oh my! Smell those muffins, fresh from the oven and ready to serve! They look delicious dear. Here is some money for your trouble. Ah finally, fresh bread! All right let me get this on the shelves right away. Here is your payment.

speaker: Heidi Grainsifter
dialog: Commander Kane Bayle is the commander of all the Qeynos Guard. He is second only to his brother, Antonius Bayle. His post is in the guard house at the city gates. Mind you, do not bother him, he has a bit of a temper. The Circle of the Unseen Hand? I have heard nothing of them. Are they some sort of performing magic troupe?

speaker: Shenro Kazpur
dialog: Greetings, and welcome to my shop. I am humble merchant Shenro Kazpur of the Silent Fist Clan. Ahhh, Lu'Sun is a master of many skills. He has traveled all of Norrath, studying various techniques and disciplines. Now, he runs this small guild and passes on his knowledge to those willing to devote their lives to our cause.

speaker: Unsar Koldhagon
dialog: Hello, Ah, Unsar's Glory. . . Mind you, I haven't perfected it yet, but bring me some [rat whiskers], [snake scales], and two [bat wings], and I'll show you how it works. They seem to be very plentiful right outside the city gates. All right, check this out. . . Maybe one day you'll be good enough to do this, too

speaker: Barthal
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! Hail, Please do not request donations or handouts. I have no extra money. If I did I would rent a shop. Heh.. Why don't you try Freeport.. or Erudin.. ANYwhere but here!

speaker: Guard Bixby
dialog: Blast those dogs! The Sabertooths is what they call themselves around here. They have a nest, or whatever you call it, up there east of Surefall Glade. We can always use help here, keeping those dirty gnolls away from our fine city. Talk to Captain Tillin in South Qeynos and tell him you want to join the fight.

speaker: Guard Monroe
dialog: Blast those dogs! The Sabertooths is what they call themselves around here. They have a nest, or whatever you call it, up there east of Surefall Glade. We can always use help here, keeping those dirty gnolls away from our fine city. Talk to Captain Tillin in South Qeynos and tell him you want to join the fight.

speaker: Guard Nash
dialog: Blast those dogs! The Sabertooths is what they call themselves around here. They have a nest, or whatever you call it, up there east of Surefall Glade. We can always use help here, keeping those dirty gnolls away from our fine city. Talk to Captain Tillin in South Qeynos and tell him you want to join the fight.

speaker: Guard Philbin
dialog: Blast those dogs! The Sabertooths is what they call themselves around here. They have a nest, or whatever you call it, up there east of Surefall Glade. We can always use help here, keeping those dirty gnolls away from our fine city. Talk to Captain Tillin in South Qeynos and tell him you want to join the fight.

speaker: Guard Westyn
dialog: Blast those dogs! The Sabertooths is what they call themselves around here. They have a nest, or whatever you call it, up there east of Surefall Glade. We can always use help here, keeping those dirty gnolls away from our fine city. Talk to Captain Tillin in South Qeynos and tell him you want to join the fight.

speaker: Mira Sayer
dialog: Welcome looks up with tear-filled eyes and sobs uncontrollably. .sniff.. Oh, dear. We do not have the money. We were just robbed by a dreadful halfling bandit named Flynn something-or-other. He took the money we had set asisde for Antonius. Please. Find this highwayman and retrieve our tax payment. ..sob..

speaker: Byjan Timbokker
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! Greetings! Please do not ask me to donate to the Knights of Truth or the Priests of Life. I have enough gold going to the city of Qeynos with all these new taxes!

speaker: Wrin Liltin
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! How are you doing this fine day? I am sure you are an adventurer and I know adventurers are weighed down by excessive platinum coins. Why not purchase something?

speaker: Talym Shoontar
dialog: How are you, friend? It is a good day to be alive in Antonica! If you should happen to visit Surefall Glade, say hello to Hager Sureshot for me. Hager Sureshot is that know-nothing ranger in charge of the Protectors of the Pine. He thinks he can protect all of Surefall Glade. He is wrong.

speaker: Kinloc Flamepaw
dialog: Welcome to the Order of Three. I am Kinloc, Guild Master of the Magicians. This tunic of the Order is for you, wear it with honour. Study hard and master your skills, and you will become an important part of our Order. Yestura shall help introduce you to the ways of magic.

speaker: Tasya Huntlan
dialog: Hail, Taxes? Oh, that time again huh? Here you are. Give Vicus our best. I hear he is under the weather. Yes. He is staying here at the Lion's Mane Inn, but it is against regulations to give out room numbers. I can tell you that he loves to see the sun rise over the ocean.

speaker: Neclo Rheslar
dialog: A Hello, citizen... Watch your step, there have been many vicious beasts about today. Ah.. Hello friend.. So, I see Daenor sent you.. Uh huh, ok.. Here's something that will be very useful for you. Practice this well, friend.. It will help protect you in this harsh world.

speaker: Beggar Wyllin
dialog: Greetings! This is no place for a person of your standards. I am but a beggar, which is why I live among the sewer rats. It would be best if you were to leave this dangerous place. Rat King? What nonsense do you speak? That is nothing more than a child's faerie tale!

speaker: Indaria
dialog: Hello, have you seen my daughter? Yes, my daughter. She went to play near the aqueducts with her doll and now she is gone. If only there was some sign of her.. tries to keep her composure. She hands you a silver locket with great effort, and then bursts into tears.

speaker: Nax Ghruna
dialog: Come over here, bub. Listen to me, and don't say a word. That name is one that can get you killed. Hanns wants Stanos dead, Renux wants to please Hanns, and everyone else wants to keep their spinal column intact, you know? So, Good, you are smarter than you look,

speaker: Gornolin
dialog: picks an item up from the ground and says, 'Hey! Look what I found! Another piece of rubbish for my trailer... Must be my lucky day. Greetings, my fellow traveler. I am Gornolin Zot, traveling merchant of fine wares. Please. Take a look at what I have to offer.

speaker: Bassanio Weekin
dialog: picks up a discarded item from the ground and says, 'Don't people have enough respect for our grand city to not throw things onto the streets?! Go to the taverns if you're looking for talk. There shall be no donation from me. I have to look after myself first!

speaker: Gerault Takenmaul
dialog: Hello. Please feel free to browse. We make the finest boots in all of Antonica. Looking for Treant material? I do not work with such material. Ask Rodgar. I believe he is upstairs. No. I haven't the coins to spare. Perhaps you should ask Rodgar. He works here.

speaker: Raffel Minnmorn
dialog: Hello, friend. I am visiting from the plains. I hope to make a FORTUNE here in Qeynos. Gash Flockwalker is a druid from Surefall Glade. I have a special delivery for him. Pardon me. Have you seen a man named Gash Flockwalker? He hails from Surefall Glade.

speaker: Rineval Talyas
dialog: Hello, looks at 'I see. If a scribe is what you seek, then bring me the torn books of fire and ice. A donation of 1000 platinum is needed as well. The donation will be offered as a blessing to the gods. Take this book quickly and tell no one about this.

speaker: Dun
dialog: Tis good that Jinkus sent you with this poster. There has been talk of a Northman fugitive in the city. Take this list back to Jinkus so that our brethren in Halas are aware of the many criminals who have fled Qeynos and are still at large.

speaker: Eracon Krengon
dialog: Tis good that Jinkus sent you with this poster. There has been talk of a Northman fugitive in the city. Take this list back to Jinkus so that our brethren in Halas are aware of the many criminals who have fled Qeynos and are still at large.

speaker: Hadden
dialog: Hello there, traveler. I hope you weren't planning on doing any fishing here. They don't seem to be biting much lately. What few fish I do happen to catch, I usually sell to my merchant friends in Qeynos. It ain't much, but it's a living.

speaker: Gahlith Wrannstad
dialog: Greetings, I am Gahlith, Master of the Wizard's Order of the Hall of Sorcery. Here, we have devoted our lives and souls to the study of the arcane. My apprentice Kazlo Naedra shall tutor you and teach you the fundamentals of sorcery.

speaker: Nostahla Westfend
dialog: Greetings.. I am Nostahla Westfend, understudy of Gahlith, and Second Master Wizard of the Order of Three. Gahlith, I'm going down to the docks to see if Ghul has anything new from overseas today. I'll be back in a little while.

speaker: Konem Matse
dialog: Ah, greetings, young Oh I see.. Phin's always after me about something. I mean, it's not my fault the order hasn't come in yet. Hey, since I'm so busy right now, why don't you be a friend and take this back to Phin for me, huh?

speaker: Poacher
dialog: Hello, adventurer. I hope the land of Norrath has been kind to you. I myself have made a killing. Hunting is surely the sport of the rich. And if you are not rich at its start, you soon shall be, from the profit made off hides.

speaker: a poacher
dialog: Hello, adventurer. I hope the land of Norrath has been kind to you. I myself have made a killing. Hunting is surely the sport of the rich. And if you are not rich at its start, you soon shall be, from the profit made off hides.

speaker: Timvu
dialog: picks up something from the ground. What are you doing down here? This is a dangerous place. We are only here to finish off any enemies of [the Circle] who escape the sharks. What? Why the Circle of Unseen Hands, of course!

speaker: Edvard Tommels
dialog: Greetings, Oh good! I can add them to my collection. We are preparing to make books you know. Bring me any of the scrolls you find in the old Splitpaw lair, and I would be happy to reward you handsomely. Why thank you,

speaker: Ginleen Harltop
dialog: Hail! Keep all combat to the center of the ring. So says [Ebon Strongbear]. Ebon Strongbear is the current champion of the Steel Warriors. As champion, he rules over the Steel Warriors.

speaker: Pelshia Thuxpire
dialog: Heya.. If you see anything you like, I'm sure we can make a deal. Um, just fine, Knargon. What do you want now, huh? How about you go, and I'll catch up with you... never?

speaker: Fish Ranamer
dialog: Hail, Taxes?! Taxes?! Tax me right out of business is what you folks are doing! Take your taxes and get out of my pub! Unless, of course, you care to buy some drinks...?

speaker: Executioner
dialog: I have no time to talk, citizen. Please, step aside! Sir. You called for me? McNeal Jocub? You have been found guilty of crimes against the city of Qeynos. Aye sir!

speaker: Rabley Trumend
dialog: Buy something!! This is no tavern! I am not selling conversation! You must be kidding! As a merchant I pay enough in taxes. There is nothing LEFT to donate!

speaker: Tovax Vmar
dialog: What? Who the heck are you and how do you know my name?! Never mind... Just leave me alone! S'ragg is going to have my head for losing that letter...

speaker: Misty Storyswapper
dialog: Nice to meet you, I will tell you no more. I do not wish to end up boxed and buried. Just stay away from Guard McCluskey. He is no honorable man.

speaker: Argyn Shieldstan
dialog: You have set foot upon the sacred grounds of the Shrine of Bertoxxulous. You had best have business here or this dirt shall become your grave.

speaker: Fhara Semhart
dialog: Taxes? How can I afford more taxes? You tax us poor merchants right out of Qeynos. Freeport is looking better and better every day.

speaker: Wylin Dodmil
dialog: Excuse me, but you should not be down in the pens. There are very dangerous creatures kept here. For the gladiator games, you know.

speaker: Rendallen Ironforge
dialog: Greetings and welcome to Ironforge Weapons! Be sure to purchase Ironforge weapons, they are assured not to break during combat.

speaker: Rucio Divella
dialog: Hello, fellow citizen! Welcome to the Office of the People. What can I do for you?

speaker: Nug Rellash
dialog: Hello, Let's see here... That is good for one free ale. Here you go. Enjoy it!

speaker: Ziska Ironforge
dialog: Welcome to our home of the finest jewelers in all of Norrath. I know nothing.

speaker: Buzzlin Bornahm
dialog: Ahoy there, swabby! This here side of the world sure is beautiful, ain't it?

speaker: Drawna Opimsor
dialog: Yeah, it seems hard to come by lately. We're all out of rat whiskers.

speaker: Tovax Vmar
dialog: Huh? What the... oh, no... S'ragg! PLEASE! NO! Bertoxxulous save me!

speaker: Varsoon
dialog: How dare you address me thus?! Your ignorance shall bring your doom!

speaker: Anehan Treol
dialog: Ah.. Hello there.. Oh, report time already again? Yeah, here ya go,

speaker: Omorb
dialog: Get out of here! You are lucky Omorb didn't crush you by mistake!

speaker: Soulbinder Novalu
dialog: Greetings Binding your soul. You will return here when you die.

speaker: Crumpy Irontoe
dialog: Bug off! Unless you want an Irontoe boot to caress your head?!

speaker: Quinon Hulleaf
dialog: Eh?... <yawn>... Ah good evening to you, too, Ah, hey,

speaker: a gelatinous cube
dialog: absorbs a discarded item into its corrosive body.

speaker: Guard Sylus
dialog: Come along, fiend. We haven't far to go.
